note: Ported concept from srednoff-os (Codex sibling project), adapted to this repo's existing budget vocabulary (lean/balanced/deep/turbo) and mode-router.ps1/.sh. legacy_mode is the pre-v1.15 3-way classification (normal/deep/turbo) that mode-router still also emits, so any consumer written against the old contract keeps working unchanged.

modes.name: fast
modes.description: Small low-risk changes where speed and context economy matter most.

modes.name: standard
modes.description: Normal implementation and review work with balanced quality and cost.

modes.name: production
modes.description: Launch, deploy, release, SEO/PPC/growth, mobile, 3D, or other production-facing work.

modes.name: critical
modes.description: High-risk security, auth, payments, database, migration, or audit work.

turbo_override.name: turbo
turbo_override.note: TURBO raises useful validation depth. It does not bypass destructive/paid/production/secret-sensitive/license-sensitive/irreversible-action confirmation rules.
